title: Neuburgia novocaledonica
taxonomy: Neuburgia novocaledonica is classified within the family Menispermaceae and the order Ranunculales. This plant is native to New Caledonia, and its classification is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships. Neuburgia novocaledonica belongs to the genus Neuburgia, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The family Menispermaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as moonseed or menispermaceae. The order Ranunculales includes various families of flowering plants, such as the Ranunculaceae, Berberidaceae, and Menispermaceae.

intro: Neuburgia novocaledonica, also known as New Caledonian Neuburgia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine, offering health benefits such as antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The bioactive constituents of Neuburgia novocaledonica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neuburgia novocaledonica is commonly prepared into a tincture, decoction, or infusion, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
